query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A black and white photograph of a wire mesh screen filling the frame, with shapes visible behind the grid pattern. In the upper center, rounded and vertical forms suggest a standing figure beside a structure with rectangular bright areas resembling windows or door panels. Organic shapes in the lower left appear to be foliage or draped fabric. The mesh creates a regular grid pattern across the entire image, and the tonal range extends from deep black to bright white.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A black and white photograph captured through a wire mesh screen, exhibiting heavy film grain and intentional blur that smears textures and renders the background subjects with absent detail. The grid pattern dominates the composition, reducing the vaguely discernible figure, rectangular bright panels, and organic lower-left forms behind it to abstract shapes and gestures. The obscured, ghostly presentation creates an atmosphere of unease and distress, prioritizing surface texture and pattern over recognizable subject matter.
